job_statement: Implement progressive delivery strategies to reduce deployment risk
context: When releasing new product updates to production environments
goal: So I can expose new versions to only a subset of users initially and verify behavior before broader rollout
evidence: using-argo-rollouts-for-progressive-deployment-delivery.adoc -> Introduction section, lines 12-13: 'Progressive delivery is the process of releasing product updates in a controlled and gradual manner. Progressive delivery reduces the risk of a release by exposing the new version of a product update only to a subset of users initially.'
notes: Source content from assembly introduction. Progressive delivery is core value proposition of Argo Rollouts.

job_statement: Automate deployment rollouts and rollbacks based on metrics and policies
context: When managing application deployments with different versions
goal: So I can drive automated progression or rollback decisions without manual intervention
evidence: using-argo-rollouts-for-progressive-deployment-delivery.adoc -> Introduction section, lines 17-19 [module: modules/gitops-benefits-of-argo-rollouts.adoc, type: CONCEPT]
notes: Argo Rollouts provides automation and metric analysis to support progressive delivery and drive automated rollout/rollback decisions. Integrates with metric providers like Prometheus.

job_statement: Control traffic distribution between different application versions during updates
context: When deploying new versions of applications
goal: So I can gradually shift traffic from old to new versions while monitoring behavior
evidence: using-argo-rollouts-for-progressive-deployment-delivery.adoc -> Introduction section, lines 18-19: 'manage multiple replica sets that represent different versions of the deployed application. Depending on your deployment strategy, you can handle traffic to these versions during an update by optimizing their existing traffic shaping abilities'
notes: Core capability of Argo Rollouts for progressive delivery. Enables fine-grained traffic control.

job_statement: Simplify setup of advanced deployment strategies
context: When implementing blue-green or canary deployments
goal: So application teams can define rollout strategies declaratively without managing multiple deployments and complex automation
evidence: modules/gitops-benefits-of-argo-rollouts.adoc -> Benefits section, lines 9: 'With Argo Rollouts, you simplify this process by allowing application teams to define their rollout strategy declaratively. Teams no longer need to define multiple deployments and services or create automation for traffic shaping'
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-benefits-of-argo-rollouts.adoc (CONCEPT). Key benefit is simplification of complex deployment patterns.
